titleStmt.title: SII 25.255: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 255. (A.R. No. 255 of 1908.) KURUSPĀL, BASTAR STATE, CENTRAL PROVINCES.

summary.p: Slab fixed on the wall of palace building (Findspot: A tank known as Choryā-tarāi).

This Sanskrit inscription in Nāgarī characters furnishes the details of date viz., Śaka 1019, Īśvara, Saptamī, Saturday and Svāti-nakshatra. The cyclic year Īśvara occurred in the Śaka year 1019 and not in Śaka 1020 as suggested by Hiralal (Ep. Ind., Vol. X, p. 38). The tithi Saptamī combined with a Saturday and the Svāti-nakshatra occurred in the bright fortnight of the Śrāvaṇa month. The above details of date correspond to 1097 A.D., July 18, Saturday.

The record belongs to the victorious reign of the illustrious king Sōmēśvaradēva who belonged to the Nāga family. He was the lord of Bhōgavatī, whose crest was a cow and tiger, who was the Sun to the lotus of the Chhinda family, who was a rāja-vidyā-nidhāna (store-house of statesmanship), samasta-jagad-āśraya (the shelter of the whole world), kōdaṇḍa Dhanaṁjaya (Arjuna in the use of bow), pratigaṇḍabhairava (terrible to his opponents), raja-Purūrava (Purūravas among kings), bhōgavidyādhara (demi-gods in enjoyments), vīra-Lakshmī-dhara (brave like Nārāyaṇa), mahim-Mahēṁdra (glorious like Indra), satya-Hariśchaṁdra, Vindhyāvāsinīdēvī-varaprasāda (who had the favour of the blessings of goddess Vindhyāvāsinī), etc.
It records the gift of eleven gadyāṇas for maintaining a lamp to the deity Lōkēśvara by the residents of a village not named.
This was written by Chaṇḍēśvara-paṁḍita. It was caused to be engraved by Mitradēva Sūtrakāra. Raṇadhavaḷa (Dugarā)ṇaka is also mentioned.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti sahasra phaṇāmaṇikiraṇa nikarāva bhāsura
nāgavaṁśodbhava bhogavatīpuraveśvara viśadaja
ya paṭu paṭāha gāṁbhīryya dhavālālaṁkārita daśā diśāṁtarā
la dhenu vyāghra lāṁcchana cchiṁdakula kamala bhāskara mahāmahe
śvara caraṇa kaṁja kiṁjalka puṁja piṁjarita bhramarāyamāna
rāja vidhyā nidhāna samasta jegadāśrayaṁ kodaṁḍa dhanaṁjayaṁ rā
jādhirāja sahaja manojaṁ pratigaṁḍa bhairava rāja pururava bho
ga vidyādharaṁ vīra lakṣmīdharaṁ mahimā maheṁdra satya hariścaṁdraṁ
madana maheśvaraṁ nija bhujoparjita
śvara śrī pana vindyavāsinī
devī varaprasādaṁ śrī someśvaradeva pravardhamāna
śake saṁvāt 1016 Īśvaranāma saṁvatsare
sita saptamyāṁ saniścara Read śanaiścaradine svāti nakṣatre lokeśvarasya
rāya dīpakaṁ sarvvajanena maṇṭhāpahaṁ Read malāpahaṁkṛtvā Ai
kādaśa gadyānakā There is a symbol after in the word ‘gadyālanakā’grāha Itvā dataṁ lokeśvarāya
lākeśvarasya Agreṇa dīpakaṁ yo dadāti vaiviśiṣṭaṁca
pradīpena śivalokaṁ brajennaraḥ tapasvī brahmaṇaścai
va sādhuputraiḥ kṛtodyamamaṁ Īduśaṁ pālyate Read ‘pālayeṁdyastu’yastu tasya
punyamanaṁtakaṁ . Asya dharmma vināsāya kurute yo ma
donkaṭaḥ brāhmaṇaiḥ Read ‘brāhmaṇānām’koṭighātaṁ liṁga bheda bhavet
yāvannabho tiṣṭhati caṁdrasūryo meruḥ kṣitirbhū dharatārakādyāḥ
Apāṁpatiścaiva saridvanaughaṁ tāvatsamaṁ dīpaka prasvālayaṁ Read ‘pradānoyaṁ’ This does not suit the metre.
cchaṇḍeśvara Read ‘caṇḍeśvara’paṁḍitena likhitaṁ . mitradeva sūtrakāreṇa U
ktīritaṁ Compare with the last line of ‘Two Kuruspāl Inscriptions of DharaṇamahādēvI’ (See, Ep. Ind., Vol. X, p. 33, Text line 26).raṇadhavala [dugarā ?] ṇaka preritaṁ
Digital edition of SII 25.255 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
